refusal_class_identifiers.summary: Refusal when later-wave claims depend on non-empty governed trust-root posture while the canonical trust-root registry remains empty.
Refusal when later-wave claims depend on stable trust-policy maturity even though trust-policy posture remains provisional.
Refusal when trust-bearing meaning is inferred from insufficient, derived, or non-promoted receipt or provenance evidence.
Refusal when shadow or parallel receipt evidence from Ζ-B3 is used to imply canonical trust-bearing truth without FULL review.
Refusal when trust-sensitive claims assume lawful cutover proof, distributed-runtime maturity, or authority handoff maturity that the repo still lacks.
Refusal when bounded review-gated work drifts into live trust, revocation, or publication execution.
Refusal when trust-bearing or containment-bearing claims imply distributed trust or authority convergence that has not been realized.
Refusal when local verifier behavior, logs, CI summaries, dashboards, or mirror views are treated as enough to establish canonical trust posture.

containment_class_identifiers.summary: Keeps trust-aware later-wave work inside explicit review posture and prevents it from becoming ordinary execution scope.
Preserves that Ζ-B3 evidence remains shadow or parallel only and must not be promoted by convenience.
Requires refusal and containment posture to remain tied back to operator transaction, receipt, and provenance continuity law.
Preserves the distinction between publication visibility posture and trust truth.
Preserves that runtime, cutover, hotswap, and distributed-authority references remain boundary constraints rather than solved proof.
Preserves that refusal and containment reconciliation does not itself admit a second later-wave family or loosen first-wave or Ζ-B3 guardrails.

review_posture_categories.summary: Allowed for doctrine, planning, and gating reconciliation only.
Required for all trust-aware refusal and containment meaning.
Required for any evidence reclassification, trust-root reinterpretation, trust-policy rebinding, receipt-promotion attempt, or runtime-boundary overclaim.
Required for any attempt to reinterpret this family as a second admitted later-wave family.
Applies to live trust execution, live revocation propagation execution, live publication execution, authority handoff, and distributed trust convergence.

trust_policy: Trust-policy presence is not execution maturity.
Provisional trust-policy posture is itself a reason to refuse later-wave overclaim.
This artifact may interpret policy insufficiency but may not mutate trust policy or trust roots.
revocation_and_receipts: This artifact remains downstream of trust execution and revocation continuity plus operator transaction and receipt law.
Refusal posture must remain attributable, typed, and reconstructable.
Local logs, CI traces, dashboards, or mirror views may not replace canonical receipt-bearing truth.
anchorization: Ζ-B3 shadow or parallel evidence posture remains intact.
Refusal may block any attempt to promote anchorization into canonical live receipt truth without FULL review.
Containment may preserve blocked or refused anchorization posture without turning it into a second admitted later-wave family.
runtime_and_distributed_authority: This artifact remains downstream of distributed authority foundations, hotswap boundaries, and bounded runtime cutover proof rehearsal.
It may reference those boundaries as blockers but may not claim they are solved.
Containment must stop trust-sensitive law from becoming a back door into authority handoff or distributed-runtime realization.

invalidity_failure_categories.summary: A trust-sensitive claim hid or erased the fact that the canonical trust-root registry remains empty.
Provisional trust-policy posture was misrepresented as stable execution maturity.
Shadow or parallel receipt evidence was promoted without the required FULL review.
Local verifier behavior, logs, or dashboards were presented as canonical trust truth.
Refusal or containment posture lost its canonical linkage to receipt and provenance continuity law.
Publication visibility changes were treated as proof of trust truth.
Trust-sensitive claims exceeded current runtime, cutover, or distributed-authority maturity.
This family was used to imply distributed trust or authority convergence that has not been realized.
A still-blocked family state was rewritten as if it were later-wave admitted.
Review-gated reconciliation drifted into ordinary execution scope.

canonical: upstream trust, release, receipt, runtime, and checkpoint law
explicit FULL-review decisions that approve or refuse reclassification

prohibited_shapes: treating refusal as live trust execution
treating Ζ-B3 shadow or parallel evidence as canonical trust-bearing receipt truth without review
using review-gated reconciliation to smuggle in a second later-wave family
using local verifier behavior as proof of distributed trust convergence
using publication visibility changes as proof of trust truth
using trust-aware refusal to rebrand broader zeta realization as bounded work

downstream_refinement_notes.title: blocked and future-only zeta frontier remains handoff material after mega validation
